data.title: BK Earnings: Bank of New York Mellon Stock Slips Despite Beating Q4 Estimates
data.description:
Bank of New York Mellon (BK) stock was down on Tuesday following the release of the financial company's Q4 2025 earnings report. It reported diluted earnings per share of $2.02, compared to Wall Street's estimate of $1.91. This also represented a 31% increase year-over-year from the $1.54 per share reported in the fourth quarter of 2024.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Bank of New York Mellon reported revenue of $5.2 billion in Q4 2025, which was better than analysts' estimate of $5.14 billion for the period. The company's revenue also grew roughly 8% year-over-year from $4.8 billion. The company's revenue growth was powered by a 5% increase in fee revenue, boosting it to $3.7 billion in Q4 2025 from $3.51 billion in Q4 2024.

Bank of New York Mellon stock was down 1.38% in pre-market trading on Tuesday, following a 1.36% rally yesterday. The shares have increased 3.94% year-to-date and 58.89% over the past 12 months.
data.author: William White

data.title: Goldman Sachs (GS) Is About to Report Q4 Earnings. Options Traders Are Expecting a 4.08% Move in GS Stock
data.description:
Goldman Sachs (GS) is scheduled to announce its results for the fourth quarter of 2025 on Thursday, January 15. GS stock has rallied about 70% over the past year, driven by the strength in the bank's investment banking and bond trading revenues. According to TipRanks' Options Tool, options traders expect about a 4.08% move in either direction in GS stock in reaction to Q4 2025 results.

data.title: JPMorgan Earnings: JPM Stock Pops despite Apple Card Deal Hitting Q4 Profits
data.description:
Shares of JPMorgan Chase (JPM) jumped over 1% in pre-market trading on Tuesday after the bank's profit dropped in Q4 from taking a one-time charge from its deal to take over the Apple (AAPL) credit card partnership. The bank reported a profit of $13 billion, or $4.63 per share, for the fourth quarter, down from $14 billion, or $4.81 per share, a year earlier. Profits increased to $14.7 billion, or $5.23 per share, excluding a one-time item, sending shares up.

data.title: Looking for Exposure to CoreWeave Stock (CRWV) in 2026? Here's How to Buy Without the Risk
data.description:
CoreWeave (CRWV) enters 2026 in a strong position as a fast-growing cloud provider. The company benefits from surging demand for GPU-accelerated computing across AI, machine learning, and high-performance workloads. Its deep partnership with Nvidia (NVDA) and rapid build‑out of data‑center capacity give it an edge over traditional cloud giants that are slower to adapt. Thus, to gain exposure to CRWV stock, investors may consider investing in these two ETFs: ARK Next Generation Internet ETF (ARKW) and Renaissance IPO ETF (IPO).

ARK Next Generation Internet ETF (ARKW)

The ARKW ETF is an actively managed fund that invests in companies shaping the future of the internet. It is run by Cathie Wood's ARK Invest and focuses on big innovations like AI, blockchain, and cloud computing. Importantly, CRWV stock accounts for 1.56% of the ARKW ETF's total holdings.

Apart from CoreWeave, some of the top stocks in the ARKW ETF are Tesla (TSLA), AMD (AMD), and Roku (ROKU). Overall, the ETF has $2.9 billion in assets under management (AUM). Also, it has an expense ratio of 0.76%. Over the past six months, the ARKW ETF has declined 0.62%.

On TipRanks, ARKW has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on 33 Buys and 13 Holds assigned in the last three months. At $174.51, the average ARKW ETF price target implies 13.14% upside potential.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Chevron (CVX) Competes for $22 Billion Lukoil Asset Sale as U.S. Deadline Nears
data.description:
Several major investors are racing to buy $22 billion of global oil assets owned by Russia's Lukoil, as a U.S. Treasury deadline nears. The leading bidders include private equity firm Carlyle Group Inc (CG), a group led by Chevron Corporation (CVX), and Abu Dhabi-based International Holding Company.
The sale is taking place in the midst of Western sanctions that prevent Lukoil from holding these assets following Russia's war in Ukraine. As a result, any deal must win approval from the U.S. Treasury before it can close.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The assets are spread across several regions and include oil fields in Iraq, Azerbaijan, and Kazakhstan. They also include refineries in Italy, the Netherlands, Bulgaria, and Romania, as well as more than 2,000 fuel stations across Europe and the U.S. Earlier this month, reports indicated that Chevron plans to team up with Quantum Capital Group and divide the assets after the purchase. At the same time, Carlyle is working with Goldman Sachs (GS) as it prepares its own bid.

Meanwhile, CVX shares rose 0.14% on Monday, closing at $162.34.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Citi Upgrades Palantir Stock (PLTR) to Buy, Sees Explosive 80% Revenue Growth
data.description:
Citi's four-star-rated analyst Tyler Radke has upgraded his rating on Palantir Technologies (PLTR) from Hold to Buy, citing an expected explosive 80% revenue growth in 2026. Meanwhile, Radke raised his price target from $190 to $235, predicting an upside of over 30%. He also highlighted the upcoming Q4 results as a key catalyst, signaling growing confidence in the company's AI-driven data analytics business.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Why Citi Turned Bullish on Palantir

Radke pointed to growing enterprise AI spending and rising government modernization budgets as the main reasons for turning bullish on Palantir.

According to Radke, Palantir's enterprise use cases are gaining momentum, based on its recent CIO survey and industry discussions. He also highlighted a "supercycle" for the government segment, driven by accelerating defense budgets and modernization priorities. He further noted that delayed 2025 government projects provide additional upside.

As a result, Citi expects fiscal 2026 government revenue to rise about 51% year-over-year, roughly 8 percentage points above consensus, with upside potential exceeding 70%. Overall, the firm projects Palantir could deliver 70%–80% revenue growth in 2026.

Citi Expects Strong Q4 Results in February

Radke expects Palantir to deliver strong fourth-quarter results when it reports its Q4 2025 earnings on February 2, 2026. He believes Palantir will continue its strong growth into Q4 after two consecutive quarters of double-digit gains. Radke also said 2026 revenue estimates could be raised, thanks to robust U.S. commercial deals and positive feedback from customers using its AIP platform.

Below is a screenshot showing how Palantir's remaining performance obligations (RPO) have grown over the past few quarters, highlighting the company's expanding backlog of contracted revenue. This growth reflects strong demand for Palantir's enterprise and government AI solutions.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: 3 Best Value Stocks with Over 30% Upside in 2026, According to Analysts
data.description:
Investors looking for undervalued stocks in the current volatile market may want to pay close attention to Wall Street's latest picks. These stocks stand out for their solid fundamentals, attractive valuations, and catalysts that could drive meaningful gains in the months ahead.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Before we go further, let's define value stocks. These are companies that look undervalued compared to their true worth, trading at lower price-to-earnings or price-to-book ratios. When the market eventually recognizes their real value, they can deliver strong gains.

Now, let's check the three value stocks that analysts have spotlighted. Each carries a "Strong Buy" consensus rating but also offers more than 30% upside potential from current levels.

Here Are This Week's Stocks

Encompass Health (EHC) – The company provides inpatient rehabilitation services through its nationwide network of rehabilitation hospitals. It has a Strong Buy analyst consensus rating and an average price target of $143.33, implying a 39.48% upside potential from the current levels. The company's P/E of 19.45x reflects a 29.7% discount to the Healthcare sector's median of 27.7.

Last month, Encompass Health reaffirmed FY25 revenue guidance of between $5.905 billion and $5.955 billion, and EPS in the range of $5.22 to $5.37

Smithfield Foods (SFD) – This global packaged meats and fresh pork producer is best known for its pork products and vertically integrated supply chain. Its average price target of $29.75 implies a 36.97% upside potential from the current levels. SFD stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating. Trading at a P/E of 9.8x, the company is valued 53.6% below the Consumer Defensive sector's median multiple of 21.13.

The company raised its fiscal 2025 profit outlook by $25 million, citing strong year‑to‑date results and growing confidence in its strategy.

SkyWest (SKYW) – SkyWest operates a regional airline in the United States. It has a Strong Buy analyst consensus rating and an average price target of $131.00, implying nearly 30% upside potential from the current levels. With a P/E ratio of 9.7x, the stock is priced at a 62.1% discount to the Industrials sector's median of 25.61.

Last month, Citi's John Godyn initiated coverage on SkyWest stock at Hold with a $112 target, saying an "elongated mid‑cycle" from 2026 favors large supermajor airlines over legacy low‑cost carriers.

What Is TipRanks' Smart Value Newsletter?

TipRanks' Smart Value Newsletter helps investors identify high-potential value stocks with strong fundamentals and long-term growth potential, based on TipRanks' data and analysis. The newsletter, published weekly, includes macroeconomic, market-wide, and company-specific analysis to help investors understand the trends that affect value investing.
